title: Chomelia anisomeris
taxonomy: Chomelia anisomeris is a species in the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as madder or coffee family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Chomelia anisomeris is classified under the subfamily Ixoroideae. The Ixoroideae subfamily is characterized by its unique flower structure and is found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Chomelia anisomeris, specifically, is native to Central and South America, where it grows as a shrub or small tree. As a member of the Ixoroideae subfamily, Chomelia anisomeris is closely related to other plants in the same subfamily, such as Gardenia and Pentanisia.

side_effects: Chomelia anisomeris is reported to have potential side effects due to its toxic alkaloids, particularly secologanin and loganin. intro: Chomelia anisomeris is a plant species native to tropical regions, commonly known as Wild Coffee or Anisomeris. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function.
